# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash -x
# Pipe and Process Data with Temporary Files (temp files) 
OUTPUT_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp" # predictable temp file name pattern; $$ is process id of script, useful for multiple processes to share the same temporary space. This might differ in different systems due to various conventions and naming patterns used by Unix based system (like /dev/{fd,ppp},/proc/, etc.)
DATA="My Data To Process Here!" # Sample data that will be written into output file
echo ${DATA} > "$OUTPUT_FILE"  # Writing to the temp File. Replace DATA with your own command or code you want run on this process and write result in OUTPUT FILE, which is /tmp/myapp-$$.temp for predictable names across multiple runs of same script
# Here replace following lines as per requirement... You might have a data processing operation here such as sorting the file contents using 'sort' command etc., you can use it to read from and write into OUTPUT_FILE 
RESULT=$(cat "$OUTPUT_FILE") # Reading Data From Temp File. This will capture content written in outputfile, which is essentially your processed data set after processing done on provided DATA by preceding lines... Replace RESULT with appropriate operation based upon requirement for the temp file contents; use it to print or process OUTPUT FILE's result
echo "The Result of Processing Is : $RESULT" # Print out final results. Use 'cat/ echo, sed and other command line utilities here if required by your operations in outputfile content above... 
# Removing Temporary File after use (optional) - it can be skipped while running the pipeline as this is done automatically when temp file goes out of scope or script terminates. Uncomment following lines for deletion once you are finished with processed data set:
rm "$OUTPUT_FILE"  # remove temporary file only if not needed after use, otherwise delete it to free up space and save time in long running pipelines like above example   ;; this line will skip the rm command by itself which is causing an error. You can either comment or uncomment these lines as per your requirements
